name: plan-runner
description: An SDK chat panel that autonomously drives a master-plan project — one fresh Claude context window per step, right inside VS Code.

planRunner.pauseThresholdPct.description: Pause the loop when account usage reaches this % (applies to all windows).

planRunner.usagePollSeconds.description: How often (seconds) to poll `claude /usage` for the meter.

planRunner.finalizeQuietSeconds.description: After a step finishes, wait for this many seconds of no activity before starting the next step — gives the session time to commit/push and leaves its summary on screen. 0 = advance immediately.

planRunner.maxTurns.description: Optional per-step turn ceiling. When >0, a step that loops tools without ending its turn is halted at this many turns and drops to needs-you. 0 = no cap (default).

planRunner.maxStepsPerRun.description: Optional ceiling on how many steps one ON run completes. When >0, the run stops cleanly between steps once this many have finished. 0 = unlimited (default).

planRunner.stopAtTime.description: Optional wall-clock stop time in 24h "HH:MM". When set, the run stops cleanly between steps at/after this time (next occurrence if it's already past when the run starts). Empty = off (default).

planRunner.stallNotifySeconds.description: Optional mid-turn stall watchdog. When >0, a live turn that produces no output for this many seconds fires one OS notification + panel line so a hung stream is visible before morning. Notify-only — the turn is never killed or altered. 0 = off (default).

planRunner.autoSkipQuestionSeconds.description: Optional auto-skip for question cards (AskUserQuestion only, never permissions). When >0, an unattended question card shows a countdown and auto-Skips at zero so overnight runs never strand on a question. Any interaction with the card cancels the countdown. 0 = off (default).

commands.title: Plan Runner: Toggle Auto-Run for this Workspace
Plan Runner: Reinstall master-plan + next-step Skills

scripts.vscode:prepublish: echo 'no bundling yet — ships src + node_modules'
